programme.sub-title: Pour ou contre obliger les fonctionnaires fédéraux à revenir au bureau 3 jours/semaine?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Déborah Cherenfant, Geneviève Pettersen, Jacinthe-Eve Arel et Rodolphe Husny.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Devrait-on interdire les rodéos au Québec?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : François Lambert, Déborah Cherenfant, Me Anne-France Goldwater et Victor Henriquez.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Pour ou contre les chiens sur les terrasses des restaurants?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Meeker Guerrier, Valérie Beaudoin, Victor Henriquez et notre invité le propriétaire du bar Le Saint-Bock, Martin Guimond.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Hausse de la population : devrait-ton augmenter le nombre d'élus à l'Assemblée nationale?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : François Lambert, Antonine Yaccarini, Déborah Cherenfant et Rodolphe Husny.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Pour ou contre l'abolition du prix minimal pour l'essence?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : François Lambert, Déborah Cherenfant, Victor Henriquez et notre invité l'ancien ministre libéral Robert Poëti.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Prévention des noyades : devrait-on rendre les cours de natation obligatoires?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : François Lambert, Victor Henriquez, Déborah Cherenfant et Rodolphe Husny.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Croyez-vous que la cause indépendantiste peut connaître un regain auprès des jeunes?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : François Lambert, Antonine Yaccarini, Victor Henriquez et Déborah Cherenfant.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo
programme.sub-title: Devrait-on encadrer la vente de biens usagés?
programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : Jacinthe-Eve Arel, Antonine Yaccarini, Me Nada Boumeftah et Geneviève Pettersen.

programme.title: Les débatteurs de Noovo

programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : François Lambert, Antonine Yaccarini, Victor Henriquez et Rodolphe Husny.

programme.desc: Nos débatteurs : François Lambert, Déborah Cherenfant, Victor Henriquez et Meeker Guerrier.

programme.title: American Yakuza 2: Back to Back

Meanwhile, Koji (Ryo Ishibashi), a Yakuza killer in town to meet with the local Mafia, turns the tables on a deranged terrorist who threatens to bomb an eatery. programme.title: Bruce Willis: All American Hero
programme.desc: Bruce Willis is one of the all-time greats. For the Hollywood action hero with an incredible body of workout was his portrayal of police officer John McClane in Die Hard that catapulted Bruce Willis to stardom. The action-packed flick hit movie screens across the country with a bang, later spawned four sequels, and established him as one of Hollywood's leading action hero. Bruce Willis has remained one of the most well-known actors of his generation. Known for his charm and humor, through career highs and lows Willis enjoyed much fame. With over a hundred films to his credit and many successful ones to boot, Bruce Willis is certainly an actor who has amassed a widespread fan following throughout his career. Willis showed no signs of slowing down, until a tragic announcement in 2022. Directed by Roxane Schlumberger.

programme.title: Evil Empire: Friday the 13th Chapter 2
programme.desc: At the same time, in a hospital in Pyongyang, North Korea, a female doctor discovers a phenomenon where the cells of the deceased are being revived. However, she is forced to flee to South Korea due to the authorities' pursuit. What is the key to the case that Jae-hyeok holds? Why are ruthless killers from the United States pursuing him? Why is the foreign police from Japan investigating the "Suicide Syndrome" in Korea? And why is North Korea trying to conceal the truth about a secret project from the Japanese military? A series of serial killings known as the "Suicide Syndrome" continue to occur simultaneously, and the whereabouts of both Phillip and Cheong-ha, who were investigating the case, remain mysterious. Meanwhile, the young police officer Jae-hyeok, who provided crucial clues to Cheong-ha in her time of crisis, finds himself relentlessly pursued by killers dispatched from Japan and the United States. programme.title: Handcrafted America
programme.desc: Handcrafted America - Quilt Maker, Rifles and Stone Carver: Host Jill Wagner travels the country on a quest to find talented craftsmen who still make incredible items by hand. Her first stop is Bastrop, Texas, where young quilter Maura Ambrose is fanatically dedicated to the traditions of hand sewing quilts and dying fabrics. In the hills of Morganton, North Carolina, Jill meets gunsmith Bobby Denton who creates elegant replicas of flintlock long rifles. Finally, Jill travels to Elgin, Illinois, to hang out in the workshop of acclaimed artist Walter Arnold, one of the few stone carvers in the United States who apprenticed in the marble carving studios of Italy.

programme.title: Handcrafted America
programme.desc: Handcrafted America - Sgrafitto Ceramics, Wood Tables and Steampunk Lamps: Host Jill Wagner travels the country on a quest to find talented craftsmen who still make incredible items by hand. First, in Lambertville, New Jersey, Jill visits the studio of ceramicist Katherine Hackl to learn the technique of sgraffito, the scratching of intricate patterns into the surface of pottery. Then, she's off to Kenosha, Wisconsin, where she meets Martin Antaramian, a young woodworker who is creating solid wood tables that look as if they are made of swirling fabric. Finally, she heads to Farmington, Minnesota, to see how lamp maker Shawn Carling turns castaway machine parts into hip, nostalgic lamps.

programme.title: Into The Wild Frontier
programme.desc: Into The Wild Frontier - Lucien Maxwell: Legend of New Mexico: Having explored the frontier with his good friend Kit Carson, Lucien Maxwell is eager to settle down with his family. But before he can establish a ranching empire in New Mexico, Maxwell must first survive a deadly clash with the Jicarilla Apache.

programme.title: Into The Wild Frontier
programme.desc: Into The Wild Frontier - Jim Beckwourth: War Chief of the Crow: When former slave Jim Beckwourth heads west to seek work as a trapper, he begins what will become one of the most eventful, heroic lives of all the mountain men. As a joke, one of Beckwourth's fellow trappers tells a group of Crow warriors that Beckwourth is a long-lost member of their tribe, and the adventure begins. Adopted by the Crow, Beckwourth joins them in a series of ferocious battles with other tribes, becomes a war chief, and falls in love with a fierce female warrior named Pine Leaf. He then uses his hard-won influence with the Crow to convince them to provide furs to the powerful American Fur Company. But an unexpected visit from legendary trapper and old friend Tom Fitzpatrick sets off a violent collision that forces Beckwourth to choose between the two worlds he loves.

programme.title: Wild West Chronicles
programme.desc: Wild West Chronicles - The Masterson Brothers Part 1: After Marshal Ed Masterson is shot while breaking up a barroom brawl, he hastily bans all firearms in Dodge City. Ed's swift proclamation is met with disdain by most townsfolk, including his own brothers, and places an even larger target on his back.

programme.title: Wild West Chronicles
programme.desc: Wild West Chronicles - The Masterson Brothers Part 2: After Marshal Ed Masterson is gunned down in Dodge City, his youngest brother, James, decides to follow in his footsteps and become a lawman. But when James' life is threatened, middle brother Bat Masterson races back from Arizona to protect his kin.

programme.title: Buffalo Keepers
programme.desc: Buffalo Keepers - Buffalo Keepers: Today, the buffalo is the quintessential symbol of strength, freedom, and the unspoiled American West. But in the late 1800s, the nation's buffalo were nearly wiped out by ruthless hide hunters. Those majestic herds, which once numbered in the tens of millions, were down to less than 500 animals. This is the story of the handful of determined men and women who mobilized to save the ones that were left in a last-ditch effort to stave off extinction. Ranchers like Charles and Mary Ann Goodnight rescued orphaned buffalo calves and built small herds. Former buffalo hunters like Buffalo Bill helped build public sympathy. And politicians like Theodore Roosevelt helped pass laws to protect the remaining animals. The effort would be a generational one, with buffalo herds carefully tended, bred, and passed down to new stewards when their old protectors passed away. Thanks to their efforts, there are an estimated 500,000 buffalo thundering across America's grasslands once again.

programme.title: Wagon Train
programme.desc: Wagon Train - The Nels Stack Story: Nels Stack, a war veteran, joins the wagon train. He is now a pacifist who defends against any prejudice. Stack's mild manner leads others to label him a coward. But the tides turn when his kindness gets the train out of dangerous Indian territory.

programme.title: Monster of a Problem

programme.title: Burning Up

programme.title: Natural Liar
programme.desc: First, Froy and Ronnie dodge tomatoes and pesticide when an organic farmer won't give up his tractor, and when they are in the clear, Froy confronts Ronnie about telling Sonia about the boob purchase and inadvertently reveals the Lambo repo. Next, Carlos and Matt get an offer of stardom from a slick Hollywood agent. And finally, Sonia drags more information from Ronnie and doesn't understand why everyone's going behind her back with Froy's big Lambo repo. Plus, they lose control of a Chevy Impala while repoing from a mace wielding shopper.

programme.title: Role-players and Blade Swayers

programme.title: Vampires and Drunks

Then, Sonia and Matt use the cover of night to sneak away a Nissan. Too bad the owner is throwing a party with his neck-biting, vampire-wannabe friends. And finally, Matt and Froy face the fury of a clan of drunken relatives when they interrupt a raucous family reunion.

programme.title: Who Wants Ice Cream?
programme.desc: First, Sonia, Matt, Froy, and Lou roll up and roll on a gang of brothers to gain possession of an Infinity. Next, Matt and Froy stand in the way of a drugged debtor keeping his car. And finally, it's time for some ice cream for Lou, Froy, and Sonia when they head out to repo an ice cream truck.

programme.title: Matt & Froy Auto Recovery
programme.desc: First, Lou and Sonia make their way to a home in the hills to clean a germaphobe of his car. Then, it's Matt and Froy's big repo. Having left the company to start their own, they head out after a high-end exotic car from outside a mansion. And finally, Lou, Lyndah, and Lou's cousin Rob drive out to a used car lot to pick up a few cars, but the owner has been hurt by the economy and now he's ready to do the hurting.

programme.title: Taken for a Ride

programme.sub-title: Philippines: Why is Filipino food unpopular?
programme.desc: Liesel embarks on a journey to the country of over 7000 islands, the Philippines. From the serene to the seedy, she combs through metro Manila discovering the food this culinary spectrum includes bouncing around trying the popular fast food joints, meeting the local experts such as blogger Cheryl Tiu, a taste of history at the world's oldest Chinatown, an unexpected dancing session at the Spanish haunts of Manila, to rubbing shoulders with the elite Chefs of the city. All in a quest to answer just why Filipino cuisine is unheard of!

programme.title: Maximum Foodie
programme.sub-title: Thailand: US Thai Food vs Authentic-Thai, which is the winner?
programme.desc: Liesel embarks on a journey to the country of over 7000 islands, the Philippines. From the serene to the seedy, she combs through metro Manila discovering the food this culinary spectrum includes bouncing around trying the popular fast food joints, meeting the local experts such as blogger Cheryl Tiu, a taste of history at the world's oldest Chinatown, an unexpected dancing session at the Spanish haunts of Manila, to rubbing shoulders with the elite Chefs of the city. All in a quest to answer just why Filipino cuisine is unheard of!

programme.title: Maximum Foodie
programme.sub-title: India: Can Kolkata be considered a foodie destination of India?
programme.desc: Liesel pinballs around a city known for its gastronomy, the forgotten hub of British East India trading company, Kolkata. From trying out what the bustling city's vibrant street food scene has to offer, to the lesser known gems, including, venturing into India's only Chinatown and sampling their delicacies. A tale of walking food tours, hole in the wall joints serving up North Eastern dumplings, messy fish markets, and of course, the pride of Bengal, the Hilsa fish. Is Kolkata up to par with the other major metropolitan cities of India? This episode finds out exactly that!

programme.title: Maximum Foodie
programme.sub-title: Nevada: What else is there in Nevada for foodies besides Vegas?
programme.desc: This investigative mission starts off in the sin city atmosphere of the Vegas strip. After a gluttonous stint at a generic buffet, Liesel is inspired to do a road trip to find iconic gems in the state of Nevada. From ghost towns, to monstrous foodie challenges, to hole in the wall dive bars in the middle of nowhere, this trip has it all. Liesel finds the resurgence of Basque cuisine in Northern Nevada, and learns that Reno can do fine dining as well! A trip to Virginia city also reveals that the Silver State has some haunted bars where patrons from the 1800s are rumored to be still imbibing and spooking locals! It's a road trip in every sense of the word!

programme.title: Maximum Foodie
programme.sub-title: Rome: What are the tourists missing out on?
programme.desc: Italy is a vacationer's paradise. The food, the people, the sights and sounds, the history! But are the tourists doing it right when it comes to the trying out authentic food in Rome? Liesel embarks on a mission to explore the street food of the city. With the help of her food journalist buddy, Saverio de Luca, Liesel sifts through pasticceri, food festivals, touristed hotspots, all in an effort to understand real Roman food.

programme.title: Das Boat
programme.sub-title: April Vokey and Oliver Ngy Tackle a Notoriously Complex Bass Fishery in Florida
programme.desc: Lake Okeechobee‚ Florida‚ inland sea of freshwater, grass, and largemouth bass. Das Boat dives deep into Florida with a Candian and a Californian. April Vokey meets up with big bass expert Oliver Ngy and they try to figure out one of the most notoriously complex and rewarding bass fisheries in the country. They discuss the iconography of Okeechobee, America‚ obsession with bass, glitter boats, and the founders of Captains for Clean Water explain Florida‚ water quality problems.

programme.title: Das Boat
programme.sub-title: Frank Smethurst and Mustache Rob Hit Up the Savannah River in Augusta, Georgia
programme.desc: Frank Smethurst and Mustache Rob circle back to some of their old favorite stomping grounds on the Savannah River in Augusta, Georgia. They catch some of the many native sport fish in this archetypical southern river, and set their sights on the east coast‚ most sought after migratory game fish‚ stripers. Along the way, Das Boat gets a major upgrade, and they discuss the Georgia-South Carolina rivalry, southern roots, Bartram‚ Bass, and the intertwined history of Augusta and the Savannah River.

programme.title: Das Boat
programme.sub-title: Frank and Rob Are Back on the Savannah River Huntin' Catfish and Gar
programme.desc: Mustache Rob and Frank are back on the Savannah River in Georgia, headed 50+ miles downstream into water that they haven't seen before. It‚ an exploratory mission, though the Savannah is far from wilderness, and Rob and Frank‚ journey is more National Lampoon than Heart of Darkness. At the terminus of their trip waits Wade Plantation and some heaping platefuls of southern hospitality. All Rob and Frank have to do is bring some catfish for the table and safely pilot Das Boat downstream.

programme.title: Das Boat
programme.sub-title: Steven Rinella and Grant Gulley Troll for Lake Trout on Lake Michigan
programme.desc: Steve introduces you to Dos Boat. Steve gets to South Haven, MI, where he recruits the help of local charter captain Grant Gully. Steve, Grant, and a fabricator put in 10 hours of work getting the boat ready to fish for lake trout in 100 feet of water. Grant is somewhat skeptical boat‚ ability to handle the water of Lake Michigan. They discuss Jon Gary îSteve‚ fishing mentor and the original captain of Dos Boat‚ the young anglers Jon influenced, the decline (and comeback) of the lake trout.

programme.title: Das Boat
programme.sub-title: Au Sable River Trout with Janis Putelis and Brian Kozminski
programme.desc: Steve hauls Dos Boat to Boyne City, Michigan. Janis Putelis connects with trout fishing guide Brian Kozminski. They try to get the boat prepared for a nighttime river expedition by setting it up for rowing. Along the way they discuss: the history and fish of the Iconic Au Sable river, the giant hexagenia mayflies that hatch there, whether or not fish give a damn how pretty your cast is, the load of fishing options in Michigan, and the ability to pivot when conditions suck.

programme.title: Das Boat
programme.sub-title: Menominee River Smallmouth with Joe Cermele and Tim Landwehr
programme.desc: Dos Boat crosses the Straits of Mackinaw and heads west to the Wisconsin, U.P. border. Joe Cermele teams up Tim Landwehr. They burn Tim‚ home river on a fishing show. A proposed sulfide mine threatens the future of this fishery, and he wants you to know about it. Other topics discussed: finding big fish in skinny water, how delicious northern pike are, losing a bass but catching the ‚ follower, and a hell of a lot more.

programme.title: Das Boat
programme.sub-title: Northern Wisconsin Musky with Oliver Ngy and Kevin Harlander
programme.desc: Dos Boat meets up with Oliver Ngy and Kevin Harlander in North Central Wisconsin. Oliver and Kevin are here for two things: big musky and delicious panfish. They give Das Boat a lake fishing upgrade with electronics and a powerful trolling motor. They convert a cooler into a livewell to keep bait and fish alive. They discuss whether or not it really takes 10,000 casts to catch a musky, losing the fish of a lifetime right at the boat, the figure 8, how bluegills make you feel like a winner.

programme.title: Das Boat
programme.sub-title: Big Mouth Buffalo with Ryan Callaghan and Miles Nolte
programme.desc: Ryan Callaghan and Miles Nolte travel to Minnesota to try to catch big mouth buffalo. The guys build a raised casting platform on Das Boat. They meet Alec Lackmann, the world‚ foremost expert on big mouth buffalo. Cal and Miles tag along with Alec to help release some buffalo that he was studying. Cal and Miles head over to the Mississippi River to try their luck on a population of river buffalo that don't get hunted with bows.

programme.title: Das Boat
programme.sub-title: Freshwater Drum with Danielle Prewett and Frank Smethurst
programme.desc: In the Das Boat Season 2 finale, Danielle Prewett and fishing guide Frank Smethurst explore Red Lake, MN, targeting freshwater drum, a fish locals dismiss as a nuisance. With Texan Danielle's expertise in cooking redfish and a new boat upgrade by Minnesota guide Josh Desmit, they set out to prove the critics wrong.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.desc: Steve Rinella and Chef Kevin Gillespie are back in the kitchen, this time to work some magic with meat from his New Mexico bull elk. Kevin shows shows Steve one of his favorite quick dinner fixes, Vietnamese Style Shakin Elk. The two discuss some of the mythology surrounding marinades and brining, plus Steve shows off his prized culinary possesion, his citrus juicer.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.desc: Snow Goose gets a bad rap for being less than extraordinary when it comes to table fare. In reality nothing could be further from the truth! Kevin Gillespie shows Sean Weaver a spin on an American classic, Steak Diane, that is sure to impress even the most skeptical waterfowler.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.desc: Wild + Whole is rooted in the values of sustainable eating, which often means making use of less common ingredients alongside foods in the peak of the season. Kevin and Danielle work together to transform two strikingly different ingredients, Fresh Duck Liver and Chanterelle Mushrooms, into a dish that would be right at home on the menu of any of Kevin's restaurants.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.desc: In a new take on the classic “meat and bread” combo, Top Chef finalist and Fan Favorite Kevin Gillespie shows Steve how to whip up a crispy Torta Milanese with the mountain goat Steve shot while filming MeatEater's 10th Season. The duo harvest some fresh veggies from Steve's garden while discussing the challenges that face anyone who hunts the high mountains. Kevin shares the story of his last Mountain Goat hunt while showing Steve a few chef tricks.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.desc: Kimi Werner certainly knows her way around a kitchen, and when it comes to anything seafood she is an absolute pro. But even pros can still learn something new on occasion and Kevin wants to share a method of preparing octopus with Kimi that he learned from a fellow chef, who is famous for his octopus preparations. The two work together to create a real showstopper of a dish, while sharing tips and tricks along the way that will make them both better cooks when they return home.

programme.title: MeatEater
programme.desc: Seth Morris and Chester Floyd are no strangers to a good fish fry. In this episode, they're working with Chef Kevin Gillespie to find a slightly more elevated approach to this classic preparation. The end result is sure to leave you with a strong desire to head out and catch a limit of walleye.

programme.title: In-Fisherman TV
programme.sub-title: Into the Great Wide Open: Uncharted Adventure Destinations - 2022
programme.desc: The In-Fisherman staff travels to world-class fishing destinations, as they fish for Yukon River Alaskan pike, the largest pike in North America visit a U.S. county with more than 1,000 lakes, and some of the country's the best multispecies fishing. and catch the largest white sturgeon ever seen on In-Fisherman TV.

programme.title: In-Fisherman TV
programme.desc: It's all about frenzied fishing and fine times, as the In-Fisherman staff sets hooks into St. Clair's monster muskies reveals secrets to fishing a somewhat forgotten lure option for largemouths and rides a river to fish for early season steelhead.

programme.title: In-Fisherman TV
programme.desc: Always in touch with the hottest bait happening for each new season, the In-Fisherman staff goes PowerBait rigging with skirted jigs for prespawn largemouths practices nuanced Ned rig tactics for finicky smallmouths and explains the subtleties of using what may be the best lure ever for giant pike, the Johnson Silver Minnow.

programme.title: In-Fisherman TV
programme.desc: Tackling late season pike, situational redfish, North America's Monsters.

programme.title: In-Fisherman TV
programme.desc: Targeting late season lake sturgeon, tactical troll time muskies, a read on North America's favorite sportfish.

programme.title: In-Fisherman TV
programme.desc: The In-Fisherman staff takes you to Epic Waters and shares Sporting Fishing Wonders, as they travel to Cree Lake, a world-class fly-in destination for pike and lake trout. They ponder with you: Just what makes a sportfish a sportfish? And why do some fish jump? So, too, is there action for beastly striped bass, one of the toughest freshwater fish swimming.

programme.title: In-Fisherman TV
programme.desc: It's fast action from some of the wildest and most monstrous sportfish swimming, as the In-Fisherman staff takes you to a variety of destinations across North America, beginning with three world-class spots for the magnificent muskellunge. More monsters enter the mix with quick trips for walleyes, smallmouths, and largemouths plus destination revelations and fishing tips for North America's largest and toughest sportfish, the white sturgeon.

programme.title: In-Fisherman TV
programme.desc: It's no-holds-barred fishing for smallmouths, largemouths, and saugeyes, as the In-Fisherman staff slides into the action around North America. We travel to fish the Niagara River, in New York, using cold steel for giant smallmouts. We highlight the revolutionary characteristics of Berkley MaxScent products for largemouths. And we take you to an Oklahoma reservoir for triple header action for saugeyes.

programme.title: Spencer James
programme.desc: Spencer has performed all over North America, he has won festivals, and he was recently featured on Fox's television show “Laughs.” Yet now, with the title as a Las Vegas headliner, he brings all of his talents to the stage for a once in a generation experience.

programme.title: Kendra Cunningham
programme.desc: Boston bred and Brooklyn based, Kendra Cunningham has been named as a comic to watch by Time Out New York and has been compared to Groucho Marx and Mae West by the UK's Chortle.

programme.title: Scott Novotny
programme.desc: Scott Novotny brings his high-energy goofyness to the live audience at VidAngel Studios.

programme.title: Sid Davis
programme.desc: This former airline worker hilariously unloads side-splitting baggage on stage. Sid's humor is fueled with A.D.D., a wavering ego, and a grade school teacher wife who views him as a child left behind.

programme.title: Mike Brody
programme.desc: Starting comedy on a whim at an open mic in the year 2000, Mike Brody has since devoted his life to perfecting the delivery of his high-energy act. Bucking the trend of the apathetic comedian, his genuine passion for his craft draws audience members into the unique world of Mike Brody: an affable, clever guy whose life is often derailed by his own neurotic, obsessive-compulsive tendencies. Brody has worked with Jay Pharoah, Dave Attell, Patrice O'Neal, Emo Philips, Joy Behar, Doug Stanhope, Doug Benson, and legendary street performer/madman/savant Wesley Willis. He is the official event comedian for the SyFy Channel's TV show Ghost Hunters and has also appeared on the nationally syndicated radio program The Bob & Tom Show as well as The Dan Patrick Show.

programme.title: Jasmine Ellis: Nobody's Queen
programme.desc: Jasmine Ellis shares her unapologetically hilarious takes on being a newly married millennial and why she could either be the best or worst employee in the world depending on the stars.

programme.title: Dennis Gaxiola
programme.desc: Dennis Gaxiola is one of America's funniest clean comedians. The preacher's son gained notoriety from his appearances with Jamie Foxx on Comedy Central, The Latin Kings of Comedy Tour with Paul Rodriguez, BET's Comic View, Que Locos and the 2010 season Martin Lawrence's First Amendment Stand Up. Comedy is Dennis' gift, but his relationship with Jesus Christ is the driving force in his life. A powerful testimony of redemption, healing in marriage and a personal walk with Jesus has lead to Dennis being able minister at Youth Conventions, Marriage Seminars as well as your weekly church service.

programme.title: Joe Larson
programme.desc: Joe Larson, the son of a working comedian, started comedy at an early age and has always had a knack for making light of any situation

programme.title: Josh Fonokalafi
programme.desc: Joshua T Fonokalafi is a Utah based comedian from the mean streets of Ogden, Utahs own Compton. Raised in the Northwest and the son of an Islander immigrant, Joshua tells of his hardships growing up as one of the biggest minorities in the country. Joshua was most recently featured at the Idaho Laugh Fest and is preparing to film his very own comedy special with VidAngel. He's acted in Jackie and Ryan with Katherine Heigl, and in Certain Woman with Laura Dern and Kristen Stewart. Joshua's quick wit and humor will give you an amazing abdominal workout.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: On the Go: Riding, Sailing and Soaring
programme.desc: As little ones become increasingly mobile, their fascination with all things that move intensifies too. On the Go: Riding, Sailing and Soaring exposes babies to the different ways people travel -- on land, by sea, and in the air -- while it presents them with captivating real-world images, funny puppet shows, and beautiful classical music by Beethoven, Haydn, Mozart and Rossini.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Discover the exciting world of shapes and colors! Learn about the primary colors red, yellow, and blue with real-life imagery of animals and objects. Explore colors like purple, orange, and green and see artwork by Van Gogh! Discover the circles, squares, and triangles that surround us in everyday life, and introduce baby to the world of visual arts as children and artists create their own work!

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: Discovering Shapes: Circles, Squares, and More
programme.desc: As babies develop, their understanding of the world, and how its pieces "fit" together, continues to progress. DISCOVERING SHAPES playfully introduces five basic forms circle, oval, triangle, square and rectangle as each appears in the context of toys, nature and everyday objects.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Get moving with Baby Einstein! Real, live-action footage and classical melodies engage young viewers as they discover simple words related to movement. Enjoy encouraging melodies and live-action footage of babies and animals as you learn and move alongside your favorite Baby Einstein friends. Dance along as you watch real, live action footage of animals, children, and toys.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: From the moment babies are born, they use their developing senses to discover the world. In terms of sight, bold patterns in contrasting colors or black and white are initially preferred. As for sounds, Mother s gentle voice, in any language, is music to baby's ears. Language Nursery: Voices From Many Lands acquaints your baby with the sounds of foreign language by presenting delightful, visually stimulating images accompanied by spoken passages, natural sounds and music.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.sub-title: Baby Einstein: Let's Learn about Classical Music
programme.desc: Discover instruments through narration and repetition. Real-life images and sounds will engage viewers as they dance to upbeat classical favorites from Beethoven, Mozart and more. Join your favorite Baby Einstein characters to discover what makes each instrument unique!

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Learn about the numbers 1,2,3,4 & 5 through rhyme, real-life footage, counting, stories, and repetition - accompanied by classical music and real world imagery.

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: At 12 months, babies can identify specific sounds, and they re also starting to match the sounds they hear with the objects that make them. Meet the Orchestra -- First Instruments presents your little "maestro" with a playful introduction to the sights and sounds of a real orchestra -- from the clashing of cymbals to the purring of the cello and everything in between!

programme.title: Baby Einstein
programme.desc: Cal, Neptune, Opus, Dean, Tinker and others learn about the flora and fauna of the ocean through STEAM-based principles and real-life footage.

programme.title: The Sandbox
programme.desc: The Sandbox is where our favorite Baby Einstein characters gather to play, problem-solve and have fun. Each five-minute episode explores an area of curiosity relevant to our 2-4 year old audience. Since the four weathered planks of wood that make up the structure of their sandbox are no obstacles for their imaginations, the entire universe is available to them for each new adventure.

programme.title: Don't Forget the Leaves!
programme.desc: Sid and his friends investigate and discover that leaves are food for animals, they provide shade, and they help make nutrients so flowers and trees can grow!

programme.title: The Bug Club
programme.desc: Sid and his friends use their magnifying glasses and discover ants, rolie polies, and caterpillars around the playground.

programme.title: Sky's the Limit
programme.desc: When a storm causes a giant boulder to cut off Berry Bitty City's water supply, Strawberry and the girls face the possibility of having to move away from their homes. With the help of Mr. Longface Caterpillar, the girls set out on an adventure to save their town.

programme.title: Blippi's Great Green Adventure
programme.desc: In this special episode, Blippi celebrates Earth Day with his Great Green Adventure. Caring for the environment is one of our prime responsibilities and Blippi teaches children how by taking them on a puzzle-solving adventure.

programme.title: Starlight, Star Bright
programme.desc: With time running out before the Grand Opening celebration for the new Marketplace, Strawberry's friends get the idea to invite her favorite singer, superstar CHERRY JAM, to be the guest singer. The girls are blown away when their berryjuice cell phone gets a text message that Cherry accepts their invitation! Strawberry is touched by their surprise, but reminds them that now the town and Marketplace really have to shine! The girls and Berrykins rush to turn the Marketplace Café into a suitable performance area for a big star like Cherry Jam.

programme.title: Practice Makes Perfect
programme.desc: Cherry Jam is so enjoying staying in Berry Bitty City, that she calls her managers in Big Bitty City and informs them that she is going to stay for a much needed vacation! Strawberry and the girls are thrilled that she will be with them for a little longer, and they do all they can to make their guest comfortable. But Cherry doesn't want to be treated like a guest she wants to fit in and contribute to the community. Plum suggests that she teach music lessons Plum has always wanted to learn the drums, as it would enhance her ability to give dance lessons with live rhythmic accompaniment.

programme.title: Link into the VRAINS
programme.desc: n Den City, duels are held in the highly advanced VR world Link VRAINS, which is always under attack by the Knights of Hanoi. However, a hero named Playmaker has arrived to face the Knights, capturing a program they want called an Ignis.

programme.title: Seize the Wind!
programme.desc: Playmaker continues his duel with the Knight of Hanoi using the new Speed Duel rules. Playmaker is backed into a corner by the Knight's Cracking Dragon, but a data storm soon envelopes the field and Playmaker activates his Skill.

programme.title: Shaq's Garage
programme.desc: Shaq's Garage - Ground Control to Biggie D / Saving Klaus: Biggie D goes on an unexpected trip into space, and it's up to Dawkins and Fleming to bring him back to Earth. / Shaq loans Klaus to a car museum for a benefit exhibition, but the Paq misunderstand and think that Shaq is getting rid of their friend forever! Biggie D, Himari and Gronk devise an elaborate plan to sneak into the museum to save their friend.

programme.title: Shaq's Garage
programme.desc: Shaq's Garage - Queenie For A Day / The Great Race: Queenie sees an ad on TV for a fancy, elaborate car wash and she wants to go! Unfortunately, Queenie is too big to go through it and the Shaq Paq have to find a way to cheer her up. / Klaus is boasting and bragging about all the races he won and that he is the fastest car in the world. Gronk bets Klaus that he could beat him in a race, but when the track turns out to be an off road adventure, Klaus learns a valuable lesson in humility.

programme.title: Pac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures
programme.desc: Pac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures - That Smarts!: In the absence of Sir C, Pacopolis needs a new genius, especially after Buttocks hacks Sir C's computer and builds a giant indestructible robot based on Grinder's design.

programme.title: Pac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures
programme.desc: Pac-Man and the Ghostly Adventures - Pac-Mania: Pac's enjoying being a teen idol-like celebrity, until a TV-show based on him, Cyli and Spiral makes them look like fools.

programme.title: Rainbow Rangers
programme.desc: Rainbow Rangers - What A Gas / Turtle Trek: The Rangers save the cows that are currently providing power to Preston's farm via their methane gas burps. / The Rangers save a newly hatched baby turtle who struggles to find the ocean.

programme.title: Rainbow Rangers
programme.desc: Rainbow Rangers - Tornado Hunters: The Rangers save Patty and Preston from a tornado. / The Rangers save the air in a polluted city.

programme.title: Angry Birds
programme.desc: Angry Birds - Bombina, Epic Sax-Off! & More: Fun adventure episodes from Angry Birds, Piggy Tales and Stella! In this episode watch "Bombina", "Epic Sax-Off!" and more!

programme.title: Angry Birds
programme.desc: Angry Birds - Golditrotters & More: Fun adventure episodes from Angry Birds, Piggy Tales and Stella! In this episode watch "Golditrotters", "A Fistful of Cabbage" and more!

programme.title: Talking Tom and Friends
programme.desc: Trick-or-treat obsessed Talking Ginger scares up a memorable haunted Halloween.

programme.title: Talking Tom and Friends
programme.desc: Talking Ginger's plan backfires after convincing Hank he's a figment of the imagination.

programme.title: Bakugan Geogan Rising
programme.desc: Bakugan Geogan Rising - Magnus' Journey / Beach Battle: While searching Vestroia for Dan and Drago, Magnus stumbles on a tribe of Cubbo being attacked by a huge marauding Bakugan. / The Awesome Brawlers realize that they'll have to fight underwater in the Judgement Battle.

programme.title: Bakugan Geogan Rising
programme.desc: Bakugan Geogan Rising - The Mysterious Masked Bandits! / The Mysterious Brawler: While studying Bakugan energy, Wynton detects strange energy spikes emitting from somewhere in Los Volmos. / Notorious thieves, the Mad Masks are still at large, and have set their sights on ancient Bakugan left in AAAnimus care.

programme.title: Superhero Kindergarten
programme.desc: Superhero Kindergarten - A Royal Visit: A Super K royal visit with Princess Zamunda turns chaotic when her priceless tiara goes missing.

programme.title: LEGO Marvel Super Heroes: Avengers Reassembled
programme.desc: The Avengers prepare for a party with a surprise guest when Ultron crashes the preparations by using mind control on Iron Man. While trying to rescue their leader, the rest of the team discovers that Baron Stucker, HYDRA, and Yellowjacket are in on Ultron's plans. While preparing for a party at Avengers' Tower, THE AVENGERS (IRON MAN, CAPTAIN AMERICA, BLACK WIDOW, HAWKEYE, THOR, HULK and VISION) notice Iron Man acting strangely. When Iron Man volunteers to go out and get more ice, they find it odd: when has Tony every volunteered to get anything? Suddenly, ULTRON appears on a screen: he's taken control of Iron Man! To make matters worse, Ultron takes over all the versions of Iron Man's armor and they fly off!Finally, the Avengers confront Ultron and his Ultron Minions as they attack Avengers' Tower. Ultron's ultimate plan: use all the data/stuff they've stolen to free the newly rebuilt IRON LEGION, destroy the Avengers, and control the world. Tony dons the hastily repaired Hulk Buster Armor and it's on! It's a huge battle as the Avengers try to save their beloved HQ and defeat Ultron. Will they succeed? And, more importantly, can their party be saved after Ultron tries to take down their Tower?The Avengers must now fight one of their own, and they're not sure what to do! Cap and Thor lead the charge, but can't fend off Iron Man and Ultron's Minions. Meanwhile, Black Widow, Hawkeye and Hulk track down a signal that seems to be controlling Iron Man from a castle in the “Little Sokovia” section of New York. There they fight HYDRA and BARON VON STRUCKER and learn that YELLOWJACKET is the “bug” that infected Iron Man's suit for Ultron. To help break Iron Man free from Ultron's control, the Avengers call in their own bug...ANT MAN!

programme.title: The Book Of Obsession
programme.desc: The Knights are tasked with protecting the King's valuable Ned Knights comic book during the Bookingheim Knight-a-Con. But with all the costumed kids there, will they recognize the real Jestro and Book of Monsters coming to get them? King Halbert instructs the Knights that they must take his valuable Ned Knights comic book to the “Knight-a-Kon” in Bookingham. It will be the first be first prize at the Knight-a-Kon. But the Book of Monsters wants the book, too. Having devoured Ned Knightly comic books in it's down time it has become fanatically obsessed with the storyline. General Magmar suggests they simply attack the place and take what they want, but Jestro tells them they can't: everyone will be in costume and they'll be thousands of comics there. This calls for a clever “infiltration” mission (which will also allow Jestro to see the Kon, which he's always wanted to do.) So Jestro goes into the Kon with the BOM and some monsters. And it's easy because everyone is in costume Jestro, the BOM and their monsters fit right in at the convention and Clay and crew have their work cut out for them trying to separate fiend from fan. Macy keeps going on-and-on about the story in the King's comic book as the Knights keep a watchful eye on it. Robin even shows up in the “Minitrex” – a small version of the Fortrex he built just for the Knight-a-Kon. He's convinced he can win the costume competition that is the big closing event for the Kon. At the end of the Kon there's a big awards ceremony for “Best Costume.” The prize: “Ned Knightly, Knight of Knighton, Issue #1.” The BOM must get it and scheme to get closer to it during the costume contest. He's just…about…to get the book when…the impatient Magmar attacks! Everyone thinks it's part of the big show! “Ned Knightly, Knight of Knighton, Issue #1” is tossed from person-to-person-to-person in the melee. The Knights must do something, but they're too far away from the Fortrex to download any NEXO-Power. Robin pulls up in his Minitrex they can use it to get NEXO-Power! Robin wins the best costume for his Minitrex and wins the Ned Knightly Comic which he then gives to Macy…she deserves to read it.

programme.title: Crocodile Tears
programme.desc: Cragger and the Crocodiles are taken prisoners by the Wolves. But Laval is determined to free his old friend. Laval, Eris and the mysterious ShadoWind attempt a rescue ... but something about ShadoWind isn't quite right.

programme.title: The Monsters and the Mech!
programme.desc: Hudson desperately recruits powerful help to defeat Danny once and for all - in the form of the T-Rex.

programme.title: The Snowball Effect
programme.desc: Laval and Li'Ella's relationship grows as he gives her a tour of the Lion City. But distracted, he misses a key battle against the Hunters. Tormak joins the fight and saves the day. But the Lion King Lagravis is captured and brought to the Ice Fort. Who will lead the Lions now?!?

programme.title: The Spectral Lands
programme.desc: Arin ventures into a dangerous new land to heal Ras while Nya and Kai come up with a plan to make Jay come to them.

programme.title: The Great Zane Robbery
programme.desc: Zane's search for Arin leads to an old friend but lands him in the middle of the Forbidden Five's hunt for the Prismatic Blades.

programme.title: Legendary Places, Ninjago's Most Wanted
programme.desc: Cole joins Jay and Zane inside the Nindroid to solve a mysterious puzzle and also Kai enters Zane's mind and suggests the Ninja take a break from puzzle-solving to figure out which villain is trying to hack their friend.

programme.title: Fish Story!
programme.desc: Vic and Owen find themselves, along with Sinjin, in the middle of a massive dino-fight in the old Jurassic Park Visitor Center.

programme.title: Now or Never Witch
programme.desc: As the team battles this deadly foe, the Never Witch begins her ultimate spell, one that could unravel the very fabric of the dream world.

programme.title: Never Forever
programme.desc: With the dream world on the brink of collapse, the dream chasers launch a risky plan to defeat the Never Witch

programme.title: Sheep Freak
programme.desc: The dream chasers manage a challenging incursion by returning a doom sheep to the dream world, where they discover a dark force increasing in power.

programme.title: Holiday Gift Swap Disaster
programme.desc: While the rest of the group gets creative and makes new, personalized gifts from scratch. In the end, it's a reminder that it's not about the gifts—it's about the thought and effort you put into showing you care.

programme.title: Day Of The Great Devourer
programme.desc: Disaster threatens as the ninja must fight to defeat the all consuming Great Devourer in a desperate attempt to save Ninjago.

programme.title: Art Farmer
programme.desc: An extraordinary collection of clips in all jazz genres from the most beautiful concerts and the most special festivals, performed by the finest jazz musicians.

programme.title: Charles Bradley
programme.desc: On April 26, 2012, the world-famous vocalist Charles Bradley and his Extraordinaires grace the stage of the Cigale in Paris. Success did not come easily for the American. Before Bradley (1948-2017) even considered a career as a singer, he tried to make ends meet with countless odd jobs. Only in 1996 he finally followed in the footsteps of his great idol, soul icon James Brown. Brown and Bradley shared the talent to bring an audience to ecstasy. With his mix of funk, soul, and R&B, Bradley has developed a sound of his own, in which a keen ear might find a few traces of James Brown.

programme.title: Joe Turner, Kenny Clarke Trio & Art Simmons Trio
programme.desc: This broadcast features performances of pianist Joe Turner, the Kenny Clarke Trio, and the Art Simmons Trio.

programme.title: Coleman Hawkins
programme.desc: A jazz festival named after the inventor of the instrument most associated with the genre, Adolphe Sax, would be incomplete without the man who laid the groundwork for how the instrument is played today. This must have been the reasoning of the organizers of the Festival International de Jazz Adolphe Sax in Dinant, Belgium, when they invited tenor saxophone giant Coleman Hawkins to perform there in June 1962. Appearing with a group that includes the French pianist George Arvanitas, one-time Duke Ellington bassist Jimmy Woode, and expat drummer Kansas Fields, the musician also known as ‘Bean' and ‘Hawk' serves up an hour-long set of familiar standards bookended by Hank Jones' “Chant” and J. J. Johnson's “Wee Dot”.

programme.title: Cannonball Adderley
programme.desc: An extraordinary collection of clips in all jazz genres from the most beautiful concerts and the most special festivals, performed by the finest jazz musicians.

programme.title: Wes Montgomery
programme.desc: This selection of unforgettable performances by some of the most iconic and influential jazz guitarists in history showcases these legends' unique essence. Revel in Wes Montgomery's virtuosity as he performs the captivating “Nica's Dream” in Holland in 1965, and delight in the expressive beauty of his rendition of “Here's That Rainy Day”, recorded in London in that same year. Experience the timeless appeal of Joe Pass's “You Stepped Out of a Dream” and “You Turned The Tables on Me”, recorded live in Hanover in 1975. The interplay between Jim Hall and Atilla Zoller borders on the impossible as they perform “Extensions” in Hanover in 1973. Enjoy Jim Hall's lyrical solo performance of “I'm Getting Sentimental Over You” from 1964. Witness Sonny Sharrock's avant-garde fusion of jazz and rock in an exhilarating performance of “Peanut", recorded live in France in 1970. Sharrock is joined by flutist Herbie Mann in a soulful performance of “Memphis Underground” from 1969. Immerse yourself in the magic of jazz guitar!

programme.title: Jazzmeia Horn
programme.desc: Vocalist Jazzmeia Horn's 2019 Malta Jazz Festival appearance was a celebration of jazz's future, infused with the soul of its past. Featuring Keith Brown (piano), Rashaan Carter (bass), Irwin Hall (flute and saxophone), and Anwar Marshall (drums), the young singer's performance was a reminder of jazz's enduring power to speak to the heart. Situated at the Valletta waterfront outside Our Lady of Liesse Church, Horn's concert was a truly spellbinding experience.

programme.title: Muddy Waters, Horace Silver & Dizzy Gillespie
programme.sub-title: H. Silver, M. Waters, D. Gillespie - Newport Jazz
programme.desc: The Newport Jazz Festival, first established in the North-American town of Newport, Rhode Island in the summer of 1954, has now grown to become one of the largest multi-day celebrations of jazz worldwide. It has resulted in numerous famous live albums from top-rate jazz stars, and has spawned several worldwide tours, including editions of the Newport Jazz Festival in Rotterdam, The Netherlands. In 1968, the Newport Jazz Festival in Rotterdam welcomed three talented Americans: pianist Horace Silver, guitarist Muddy Waters, and trumpeter Dizzy Gillespie, alongside their bands.

programme.title: Lalah Hathaway
programme.desc: Winner of five Grammys in four consecutive years, including the Grammy for best R&B album in 2017, Lalah Hathaway is a force impossible to ignore. The American artist has shared the scene with Prince, Snoop Dogg, Stevie Wonder, Anita Baker, Mary J. Blige, Frank Ocean and many others, an irrefutable proof of her keen musical sense and enormous talent. With a 26-year career, Hathaway is still highly sought after by the biggest names in music. See her transform the 2018 Sing Jazz festival scene into a real R&B club, where she flaunts her voice and stage presence out of the ordinary.

programme.title: Kevin John Edusei
programme.desc: In October 2020, German conductor Kevin John Edusei made his London Symphony Orchestra debut and leads the orchestra in a beautiful performance of the suite from Mendelssohn's “A Midsummer Night's Dream”, Boulanger's “D'un matin de printemps” and Schumann's 'Spring' Symphony. Edusei is regularly praised for the drama and tension that he brings to his music-making, and for his clear sense of architecture and attention to detail.

programme.title: Ivan Fischer, Budapest Festival Orchestra & Richard Goode
programme.sub-title: Beethoven - Symphonies and Piano Concerto No. 4
programme.desc: Iván Fischer leads his Budapest Festival Orchestra (BFO) in a concert program dedicated to the music of Ludwig van Beethoven. The program opens with the composer's Symphony No. 1 in C major, Op. 21. Afterward, Fischer conducts Beethoven's Piano Concerto No. 4 in G major, Op. 58, in which Richard Goode features as the soloist. The acclaimed American pianist (1943) is known for his interpretations of the Beethoven repertoire. Goode recorded all five Beethoven piano concertos with the BFO and Fischer, earning him exceptional critical acclaim and a Grammy Award nomination. As an encore, Goode performs the Sarabande from J. S. Bach's Partita in B-flat major, BWV 825. The program closes with Beethoven's famous Symphony No. 5 in C minor, Op. 67. This performance was recorded at the Béla Bartók National Concert Hall in Budapest, Hungary, on January 29, 2017.

programme.title: Luca Vignali, Angelo De Angelis, Carmine Pinto & Eliseo Smordoni
programme.sub-title: Mozart - Quintet for piano and winds, K. 452
programme.desc: Luca Vignali (oboe), Angelo De Angelis (clarinet), Carmine Pinto (horn), Eliseo Smordoni (bassoon), and Linda Di Carlo (piano) perform Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart's Quintet for piano and winds in E-flat major, K. 452. The work premiered at the Viennese Burgtheater on April 1, 1784, with the composer himself at the piano. Shortly after the performance, Mozart wrote to his father Leopold, “I consider it the best work I have ever written…”. Striking is the Quintet's unusual instrumentation, which presented the composer with a challenge “as single winds, as opposed to pairs, pose problems of blend”. Therefore, Mozart explored numerous different permutations of instruments to produce various sonorities, and used short phrases and motifs for variety. This performance was recorded at Perugia's Auditorium Santa Cecilia, Italy, on June 23, 2022.

programme.title: Leonard Slatkin, Leonard Slatkin, Orchestre National de Lyon & Baiba Skride
programme.desc: Leonard Slatkin conducts the Orchestre national de Lyon in a concert recorded at the Auditorium de Lyon in France, 2013. The concert is a tribute to the organ of the Maurice-Ravel Auditorium, which was made by Aristide Cavaillé-Coll. The program features three works which premiered on exactly the same organ: Camille Saint-Saëns' Cyprès et Lauriers and Symphony No. 3 and Poulenc's Concerto for Organ, Timpani and Strings. Soloist is Vincent Warnier.

programme.title: Julijana Sarac
programme.desc: Cinematic classical clips and energizing concert pieces featuring up-and-coming artists.

programme.title: Gabriela Montero
programme.desc: Venezuelan pianist Gabriela Montero is accompanied by the Youth Orchestra of the Americas conducted by Carlos Miguel Prieto. On the program are Maurice Ravel's Piano Concerto in G Major, as well as two of Montero's own works: the “Latin Concerto” and Improvisations. The performance took place in one of the world's most beautiful concert halls, the Teatro del Lago in Frutillar, located in the middle of the Patagonian plains. The concert hall, which opened its doors in 2010, stands on the banks of Chile's second-largest lake overlooking a volcano that is capped with snow the entire year. In an accompanying interview, Montero speaks about composing, improvising, and performing works of her own alongside those of others. She discusses her home country of Venezuela, where she no longer performs for political reasons.

programme.title: Led Zeppelin
programme.desc: This is a documentary film about the build-up, making, release, and - crucially - music contained on this most revered of albums. With the aid of rare and classic performances, scarce interviews, seldom seen photographs and contributions from those involved in the making of the record - plus the review and critique from a panel of Zeppelin experts - this program finally lifts the lid on what many believe to be Led Zep's very finest offering. With contributions from the album's engineer, Ron Nevison former Yardbird, Chris Dreja Swansong recording artist, Maggie Bell author of The Rough Guide to Led Zeppelin, Nigel Williamson Tight But Loose editor, Dave Lewis Classic Rock Magazine's Malcolm Dome renowned guitar tutor and author, Rikky Rooksby and Robert Plant biographer, Neil Daniels. This is the complete story behind what what Rolling Stone called "their Tommy, Beggar's Banquet and Sgt. Pepper rolled into one".

programme.title: Grunge
programme.desc: Grunge is very loud, very rebellious lots of very heavy guitar and fuzz effects, but in terms of its recording techniques, it's very lo-fi everything is stripped back, and really taken back to just the instruments. The lyrics tended to be about real life stuff, alienation a kind of weariness at the world a cynicism, but also a lot of humour. Grunge was the music of the underdog. There was no point aping glamour because there was no glamour in their lives, and it wasn't something that they could graduate to. So they would sit at home, play riffs, deep, big, huge riffs.

programme.title: Alt Rock
programme.desc: Emerging from the independent music underground of the 1970s it became widely popular in the 1980s. The music was unified by collective debt to the musical style and/or the independent, DIY ethos of punk rock, which laid the groundwork for alternative music. Throughout the 1980s, magazines, college radio airplay, and word of mouth had increased the prominence and highlighted the diversity of alternative rock, and with the breakthrough of Nirvana, grunge and Britpop movements in the 1990s, it entered the mainstream. With Smashing Pumpkins, Jane's Addiction and Soundgarden

programme.title: Flying Over Spanish Islands
programme.desc: In their first collaboration, cinematographer Stefan Zimmerman and Nature Relaxation™ producer David Huting present "Above the Spanish Islands." This 50-minute 4K aerial film captures Mallorca and the Canary Islands, set to ambient music by Diamans, Electus, and Michael St. Laurent.

programme.title: Flying Over Iceland
programme.desc: "Flying Over Iceland: Part I" provides a soothing aerial journey through eastern Iceland's fjords and valleys, starting at sunrise in Brei_dalsvik. Enjoy views of mists, a waterfall, and the Brei_dals' river, set to nature sounds and music by The Cynic Project, showcasing East Iceland's beauty.

programme.title: Flying Over Iceland 2
programme.desc: "Flying Over Iceland II: Summer in the Golden Circle" presents a serene aerial view of Iceland's south coast, starting at Skogafoss and moving to Vik, with black sand beaches, cliffs, and Vatnajökull glacier. Filmed in 2020, this 1-hour film features local wildlife and immersive music.

programme.title: Flying Over Oahu
programme.desc: "Flying Over Oahu" offers a 97-minute aerial journey over Oahu's iconic coastlines with nature sounds and music by Alex Smith (The Cynic Project). From Waikiki Beach to Turtle Bay, enjoy breathtaking 4K landscapes, ending with a stunning Waikiki sunset.

programme.title: Dolphin Voyage Relaxation
programme.desc: Replace your worries with wonder as you journey into the deep blue calming ocean with a pod of friendly dolphins in this new 1-hour long film. "Dolphin Voyage Relaxation" is 1-hour long and features some spectacular underwater cinematography by Peter Schneider and a synergistic blend of audio including underwater nature sounds, calming ambient music by The Cynic Project, and a subtle background layer of harmonically tuned binaural relaxation sounds by Schmidt. The result is an immersive, awe-inspiring adventure with gracefully transitions, leaving the viewer with a powerful dose of calm and healing energy direct from Nature.

programme.title: Glacial Beauty: Newfoundland
programme.desc: Head to Newfoundland's Amherst Cove to admire the quiet and glacial beauty of icebergs drifting across the bay.

programme.title: Lombok Beaches (Music)
programme.desc: Experience the beautiful beaches of an Indonesian island set to a peaceful soundtrack.

programme.title: A Day in Santorini
programme.desc: Turn any screen into a window the the Greek Islands with this immensely relaxing HD visual journey through the unique and beautiful coastal village of Oia, found on the far tip of Santorini Island. Slowly awaken your senses as the morning sun rises over the sea, and watch as day slowly passes into dusk. Distant waves break and birds chirp adding an a nice ambient music that combines to create a video that's so relaxing it's almost trance-inducing.

programme.title: A Day on the Napali Coast 1
programme.desc: A 5 hour long ambient nature film featuring the spectacular sights and sounds of the legendary 11-mile long Kalalau Trail. Featuring hundreds of breathtaking yet tranquil scenes with stereo audio, this video begins on Kalalau Beach at sunrise and progresses along the trail over the course of nearly a full day. Viewers will enjoy a countless array of views that overlook the vast blue ocean, where an abundance of wildlife and nature appear in their pristine and healing majesty.

programme.title: Te Urewera National Park: New Zealand (Music)
programme.desc: Take in the many views of Te Urewera National Park's falls, from closeups of the rapid cascades to far-away vantages from the park's highest peaks. This magical scenery also includes tree-lined trails and beautiful beach views before dusk.

programme.title: Sunset Sierra Creek
programme.desc: Relax for a full hour and transform any TV into a flowing, golden window to the high Sierra mountains with this fixed angle 4K UHD nature scene captured along the John Muir Trail.

programme.title: Zen Relaxation
programme.desc: A mix of guitar, piano instrumentals to help you concentrate and feel relaxed.
